text: ticked by much too fast, and Ronin was so still that some of the crows returned, hopping warily closer until he waved his arm to scare them off again . It was the corpse of a human woman. She was naked, and where her skin hadn’t been torn apart by hungry beaks, it was dark with bruises. His first thought was that Warlord had found Lara, but he rejected it immediately. This woman’s skin was a darker shade, her black hair stiff with blood . A synth lay on the ground near her, his head face-down over her crotch. His detached arms and legs were strewn about on all sides, tendrils of exposed wiring and tubing jutting from the jagged openings. Warlord’s symbol was upon his back; the paint had run before drying . The synth’s penis had been removed, as well, and shoved into the woman’s mouth. Crows had pecked out her eyes, but her collapsed cheekbone, too-wide jaw, and extensive bruising indicated that she’d been severely beaten before she was dumped here . She was a little taller than Lara, with full hips and breasts. Dark hair, tan skin … Ronin’s processors slowed as realization hit him, stronger than any wall of dust he’d endured in the wastes . The woman’s arms were spread to either side, palms up. Slowly, he knelt beside her, took hold of her left wrist, and turned her hand over . A jagged scar ran along it’s back, from the first knuckle of her pinky to the base of her thumb . As delicately as he could, he set her hand back down. His processors kicked into overdrive, moving fast enough that anyone nearby might have heard them whirring. He’d fulfilled one of Lara’s conditions. One less thing for him to ask about around town. One less suspicion to raise with Warlord . He stood and looked over his shoulder at the crows. They remained close, cawing and flapping their wings as they fought over whatever bits of meat they still possessed. They’d desecrated the body of Lara’s sister. Damaged it beyond what it had already suffered. Ronin looked down again. No…this wasn’t an it . This was Tabitha. A her . The synth’s facial skin was gone, leaving only dull, dented metal and shattered optics. Ronin moved the bot aside, piling the detached parts on the ground near its torso. That done, he removed the final insult from Tabitha’s mouth and gathered her in his arms; he’d see to her first, to keep the scavengers at bay . He walked west, searching for a resting place outside of Warlord’s shadow . Chapter Fifteen O nly the merest hint of orange was visible on the horizon when Ronin reached the western guard post. The gearheads, like their counterparts at the east barricade, told him Warlord didn’t appreciate visitors after dark. Ronin calmly pointed out the lingering sunlight. After staring at it for some time, the gearheads exchanged a glance with one another and waved him through . Though his pack was heavy with trade, Ronin didn’t stop in the Market. He was urged on by a different sort of weight, perceived not by his sensors, but by his processors. He had to tell Lara. There was no way to know how she’d react . Only that it wouldn’t be good . The guards at the interior gate gave him no trouble, and he hurried along the lonely stretch of road around the park. Briefly, he entertained the idea of cutting through the park to get home faster, as though surrounding himself with life would make the news easier to deliver . His memory served up the journal entry about people executed and burned in the park, and he decided to stick to the streets . As he walked, questions stacked in his mind, caught in a queue that would never clear. Why? Why, after all the senseless death and destruction the world had already faced, did there have to be more ? What could Tabitha have done to deserve it? Did anything warrant such an end ? Clearly, Warlord had a history of hostility toward humans, but why another bot, this time? The synth hadn’t merely been powered down, he’d been ripped to pieces . There was a message in the act, and though Ronin possessed the necessary information to decipher it, but meaning would not come. His processors insisted there was some sort of logical thought behind the death, deactivation, desecration, and dismemberment. But, despite his own bouts of illogical behavior lately, he couldn’t understand this act. Couldn’t derive a meaningful purpose for it . Too soon, he arrived at his residence. The windows were dark. His mild relief was accompanied by a touch of shame; if Lara was asleep, it would give him more time to determine how to relay the news to her . Ronin removed his gloves, goggles, and mask, stuffing them away, and fished the key out of his pocket. Unlocking the door, he slipped inside . His optics initialized night-vision automatically as he closed the door behind him, but something about using it seemed wrong. He overrode the change and switched on the overhead lights instead . He set his rifle down against the wall, and was shrugging off his rucksack when he noticed Lara on his periphery and looked up . She sat on the third step from the bottom. The barrel of the pistol in her hand was a gaping, black maw, swaying with the trembling of her arm . “I’m so pissed at you right now, I should shoot you,” she said, voice as unsteady as her hand. “Where do you get off leaving without a word ?” There was nothing stopping her from pulling the trigger. Though improbable, there was even a chance that she’d hit him in the optics and do considerable damage. Would it make her feel better ? “I didn’t want to say goodbye.
